title: Event Service
description: A root-level service for application-wide communication using two complementary patterns: an RxJS Observable stream (emitEvent / onEvent) and a lightweight callback-based event bus (on / broadcast). Includes predefined AXSystemEvents for locale, language, and calendar changes.

hints.title: Two APIs
hints.content: Use emitEvent() + onEvent when you need reactive RxJS pipelines (filter, merge, async pipe). Use on() + broadcast() for simple imperative callbacks — ideal for UI actions like opening a drawer.

hints.title: System events
hints.content: Prefer the AXSystemEvents enum for built-in global events (language, locale, calendar). Custom app events can use any string key in the callback bus.

hints.title: Cleanup
hints.content: Call destroy(key) to remove all callbacks registered for a given key. When subscribing to onEvent, unsubscribe on component destroy using takeUntilDestroyed() or similar.

props.name: AXLocaleChanged
props.description: Emitted when the active locale profile changes.

props.name: AXLocaleLoaded
props.description: Emitted when a locale profile is loaded.

props.name: AXCalendarChanged
props.description: Emitted when the active calendar system changes.

props.name: AXLanguageChanged
props.description: Emitted when the active translation language changes.

props.name: AXLanguageLoaded
props.description: Emitted when translation data for a language is loaded.

types.title: AXEventData
types.description: Payload structure for events emitted through the Observable stream.

props.name: type
props.description: Event type identifier (usually an AXSystemEvents value).

props.name: payload?
props.description: Optional data attached to the event.

types.title: AXEventService
types.description: Singleton service provided in root for global event dispatch.

props.name: onEvent
props.description: Hot Observable stream of all events emitted via emitEvent().

methods.content:
/**
 * Pushes an event into the onEvent Observable stream.
 */
emitEvent(event: AXEventData): void

/**
 * Registers a callback for a string event key.
 * Multiple callbacks can be registered for the same key.
 */
on(key: string, callback: (options?: unknown) => void): void

/**
 * Invokes all callbacks registered for the given key.
 */
broadcast(key: string, options?: unknown): void

/**
 * Removes all callbacks registered for the given key.
 */
destroy(key: string, callback?: (options?: unknown) => void): void

content.data:
AXEventService decouples parts of the application that need to react to global state changes without tight coupling. Core services such as AXTranslationService, AXCalendarService, and AXFormatPipe rely on it internally.

The Observable API (emitEvent / onEvent) is used for reactive updates — for example, re-formatting displayed values when the language changes. The callback API (on / broadcast) is a simpler pub/sub registry for imperative actions like toggling UI elements.

sections.title: Basic Usage
sections.description: AXEventService is providedIn root — inject it and use either the Observable stream or the callback bus.

sections.title: Observable Pattern
sections.description: Emit and listen with RxJS — the pattern used by format pipe and translation services.

sections.title: Callback Event Bus
sections.description: Register handlers with on() and trigger them with broadcast().

sections.title: System Events Reference
sections.description: Built-in events emitted by core services.

sections.title: Listening to Multiple Events
sections.description: Filter onEvent for several system events at once — pattern from AXFormatPipe.

sections.title: Custom Application Events
sections.description: Define your own event keys for feature-level communication.

sections.title: Cleanup
sections.description: Remove callback handlers and unsubscribe from Observables.

sections.title: Real-world Integration
sections.description: How core modules emit events you can listen to.
